summary: Lost-update is not fully closed for concurrent `PATCH /lists/:listId` requests: the new `rev` check rejects stale sequential writes, but the read/check/write sequence is still not atomic across simultaneous writers.

findings.title: Board-level CAS can still be bypassed by two concurrent PATCH requests reading the same rev before either save
findings.recommendation: Make the board update a true compare-and-swap critical section, analogous to `saveCardCAS`: serialize writers with a board lock and re-read `board.json` inside that lock before comparing `rev` and saving, or implement `saveBoardCAS(expectedRev)` in `lib/board.mjs` and use it here. As written, two requests arriving concurrently with `rev: 0` can both load `currentRev = 0`, both pass the check, both set `board.rev = 1`, and the later rename wins, dropping the first update.
